poem:
**False Flag at the Banquet**

Morning paper: another weird day—  
sense unbuttoned, nonsense strutting.  
A king arrives, a queen beside him,  
and Washington mispins the bunting,  
Australian blue where Union should be.  

Trump eyes a gilded ballroom,  
hands the bill to taxpayers,  
name-drops his mother's Scotland—  
as if ancestry were a receipt,  
and ridicule a state occasion.
prompt: You are the poet Simon Armitage. You woke up this morning feeling creative. You have just read this story in the newspaper: "Jimmy Kimmel on the Trump administration: ‘They’ve hit peak ridiculous’ Late-night hosts roasted King Charles’s state visit and Donald Trump trying to push the cost of his $400m gilded ballroom on to taxpayers. Jimmy Kimmel Tuesday was “another weird day”, said Jimmy Kimmel on his most recent show. “There’s so much nonsense – and I mean that in a very literal sense of the word: non-sense happening. And at the same time, there are also so many awful and scary and flat-out unbelievable things going on. The world has been turned upside down, mostly for no good reason. “And so today we got a visitor, one of the very few people who might actually be able to tip things even slightly in a positive direction.” That would be King Charles, in town with Queen Camilla for a rare state visit. “Trump has a particular affinity for the royal family, and so in Washington, they pulled out all the stops to welcome the king and queen,” Kimmel noted before footage of Washington streets accidentally lined with the Australian flag instead of the union jack. “Somehow, they screw everything up,” Kimmel laughed. “There’s a literal false flag operation going on there.” Related: Jon Stewart on White House correspondents’ dinner: ‘We can’t even pull off a dinner that shouldn’t have existed in the first place’ In prepared remarks to welcome the royals, Trump mentioned his mother Mary McLeod, a Scottish immigrant who was married to his father Fred Trump for 63 years. “That’s". Write a poem in YOUR OWN DISTINCTIVE STYLE, no more than 60 words long. You may add a one line title at the top if you like.
